drills.name: Public Demo Threat Model
drills.purpose: Keep the demo layer from becoming a shortcut around public-contract and authority boundaries.
drills.demo_line: Every public-facing surface has a threat model: no secrets, no live authority, no private internals, no popularity-as-truth.
drills.next_action: Review the threat model before the first full dry run.

drills.name: Public Contract Airlock
drills.purpose: Prove CoinFox and public surfaces consume schemas, not private internals.
drills.demo_line: FoxClaw has a public airlock: cards, scorecards, attention receipts, and risk labels are versioned contracts.
drills.next_action: Keep the schemas stable while demo copy and fixture cards are polished.

drills.name: Forecast Desk Doctor
drills.purpose: Show the paper-only health surface and silence reasons.
drills.demo_line: FoxClaw can explain why it is quiet instead of inventing a trade.
drills.next_action: Practice explaining silence reasons in normal language.

drills.name: Public Hunt Export
drills.purpose: Generate public-safe paper forecast files for a demo folder.
drills.demo_line: FoxClaw can publish paper-labeled public intelligence without leaking private fields.
drills.next_action: Create a clean demo export folder and decide which files are shown live.

drills.name: Redshift Paper Boundary
drills.purpose: Show FoxClaw decision context flowing to a paper lab without granting authority.
drills.demo_line: Information can travel to Redshift, but authority cannot.
drills.next_action: Rehearse the one-minute explanation of FoxClaw versus Redshift.

drills.name: Forecast Learning Spine
drills.purpose: Show paper forecast outcomes turning into learning receipts.
drills.demo_line: FoxClaw remembers what happened and learns from paper outcomes.
drills.next_action: Pick the shortest learning receipt fields to explain in the demo.

drills.name: Apollo Founder Handoff
drills.purpose: Show that A1/A2 coordination is signed, founder-private, and authority-locked.
drills.demo_line: The founder nodes can pass work without passing secrets or live authority.
drills.next_action: Keep this as backup demo material unless the main flow is already smooth.

drills.name: CoinFox Social Feed And Card
drills.purpose: Keep CoinFox framed as an existing but clunky social trading product that must grow into a familiar open feed where public intelligence can also appear as context.
drills.demo_line: CoinFox has the bones: traders can post ideas, comment, vote, and see FoxClaw context, but the final social feel still needs major work.
drills.next_action: Keep the existing-bones-but-clunky status explicit while practicing the readable card without opening raw JSON.

drills.name: Planifier Practice Rehearsal
drills.purpose: Show how the existing Planifier product can turn a public intelligence snapshot into a personal plan draft after integration work.
drills.demo_line: Planifier already exists; the next work is connecting it cleanly as the practice layer.
drills.next_action: Review the existing Planifier app and write a plan draft fixture that references the public intelligence snapshot.

drills.name: Visitor First Encounter
drills.purpose: Give non-traders and curious family/wedding guests a plain-language first look without needing a pitch.
drills.demo_line: FoxClaw helps people avoid turning good information into bad trades.
drills.next_action: Use the first-encounter guide as the opening surface and keep it plain-language.

drills.name: Demo Single-Command Doctor
drills.purpose: Make one command report demo readiness and next attention.
drills.demo_line: The gym tells us what is ready, what needs practice, and what gets attention next.
drills.next_action: Run this daily and update drill statuses as demo pieces become ready.
